operating_state: Net sales increased 4.5% to $159.5 billion driven by SRS acquisition contributing $6.4 billion and an additional 53rd week adding $2.5 billion, offset by comparable sales declining 1.8% reflecting decreased customer transactions and average ticket. Online sales represented 15.1% of total sales within an interconnected retail strategy. Revenue performance remains pressured by macroeconomic uncertainties and high interest rates impacting larger remodeling projects.
cost_structure: Gross profit margin remained flat at 33.4% as lower transportation costs and reduced shrink offset SRS inclusion impacts, while SG&A expenses increased to 18.0% of sales from 17.4% primarily due to higher payroll costs and deleverage from negative comparable sales. Depreciation and amortization rose to 1.9% of sales driven by increased intangible asset amortization related to SRS. Labor costs face upward pressure from competitive wage markets while commodity price volatility creates margin pressure.
material_events: Completed SRS acquisition for $17.7 billion cash on June 18, 2024, establishing the company as a leading specialty trade distributor requiring integration of products, services, associates, and information technology systems. Opened twelve new stores bringing total count to 2,347 while announcing 2.2% quarterly dividend increase to $2.30 per share. Paused share repurchases in March 2024 ahead of SRS acquisition with no plans to resume in fiscal 2025.
macro_exposures: Heightened macroeconomic uncertainties and persistent high interest rates continue pressuring home improvement demand and customer transaction volumes, with stronger U.S. dollar negatively impacting net sales by $298 million during fiscal 2024. Trade tensions and tariff risks span imports from Mexico, Canada, and China with potential significant adverse impact on product costs, while geopolitical conflicts exacerbate inflationary pressures on commodity prices and shipping expenses.
forward_outlook: Management plans approximately $4 billion in capital expenditures for fiscal 2025, representing 2.5% of expected net sales, focused on customer experience improvements, technology investments including artificial intelligence implementations, Pro capabilities development, and new store construction. SRS acquisition expected to accelerate Pro growth and establish leadership in specialty trade distribution with strategic focus on creating faster, more efficient delivery networks.
uncertainty_profile: Return on invested capital decreased to 31.3% from 36.7% primarily due to higher average debt and equity from SRS acquisition financing, while interest expense increased significantly due to higher long-term debt levels. Multiple ongoing legal proceedings including government inquiries, consumer litigation, and employment disputes create unpredictable outcomes, with pending regulatory developments around data privacy and cybersecurity requirements creating compliance complexity.
